SUPPORT OF

PiNcHoT GIVES THIS CITY THE

DENT LAST NIGHT OF GREAT

pages.raw_text:
Spokane was accorded first place
as a supporter Of the Roosevelt
conservation policy by Clifford
Pinchot. chief United States for
ester. at the banquet rendered the
press and irrigation congress Off
GERS at the Spokane club last even
ing. The Spokane board of con-
troll was the host.


Pinchot said that when he first
took up the work and came west
to survey the field, he met his
most enthusiastic support in this
city, particularly mentioning in that
relation, August Wolf of the cham
Der Of commerce publicity bureau.
and the lumbermen of Spokane.


Although standing now as one of
the few remaining major factors of
the. Roosevelt administration and
the object of combined attack by
the influences determined to erase
the effects of the Roosevelt policies
with the object of fattening private
interest enterprises, Pinchot is
calm and easy outwardly and he
was only mildly but effectively sar-
castle last night. On the toast list
he followed A. G. Campbell of the
federal department Of justice, and
one of the missionaries sent forth
in the concentrated effort now be.
ing made to shield and justify Bal
fingers course. Pinchot turned
Campbells attempts in that direc-
tion neatly and completely with a
little story illustrative of the
theory that the conservation cause
had been adjudged officially dead by
the doctors" and would naturally
be expected to accept the diagnosis
He did not state that it would be
accepted.


The incident was one of the
minor but important Developments
in the great drama now being play
ed in public affairs with the storm
center located here in Spokane
Were Roosevelt in the White
: House, there would be something
resembling an explosion from thai
direction, but the corporation attor
neys so deeply engaged in the af
fairs Of the irrigation congress
have no apparent fear of Taft, ant
Ballinger is letting somebody else
do his talking whenever possible.
